channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Putin hails Russia's test launch of a new ballistic missile
item.description: Russia test-fired a new intercontinental ballistic missile as part of efforts to modernize its nuclear forces. The nuclear-armed Sarmat missile would enter combat service at the end of the year.
item.content:encoded:
Russia test-fired a new intercontinental ballistic missile as part of efforts to modernize its nuclear forces. The nuclear-armed Sarmat missile would enter combat service at the end of the year.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
item.description: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.
item.content:encoded:
The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Some Minneapolis donors have moved on. The immigrants waiting for help haven't
item.description: During Operation Metro Surge, mutual aid efforts in Minneapolis raised millions of dollars. But with most agents gone, and with increasing fatigue from community members, funds are drying up and people are moving on. Immigrants are not.
item.content:encoded:
During Operation Metro Surge, mutual aid efforts in Minneapolis raised millions of dollars. But with most agents gone, and with increasing fatigue from community members, funds are drying up and people are moving on. Immigrants are not.

(Image credit: Tim Evans for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Staying strong, then falling apart: How a military family grapples with the Iran war
item.description: Military life has always involved some degree of uncertainty. But for many families, the fear and unknowns that come with the Iran war are new territory.
item.content:encoded:
Military life has always involved some degree of uncertainty. But for many families, the fear and unknowns that come with the Iran war are new territory.

(Image credit: Stella Kalinina for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: How the Trump administration has undermined the fight against public corruption
item.description: At least 15 former elected officials and co-conspirators with corruption offenses have been pardoned by President Trump in the last year, undermining the fight against public corruption.
item.content:encoded:
At least 15 former elected officials and co-conspirators with corruption offenses have been pardoned by President Trump in the last year, undermining the fight against public corruption.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Short flights are popular. Will they last?
item.description: Some flights are less than 100 miles, and they're the backbone of the U.S. air system, linking small towns and big cities. But short routes were in decline even before jet fuel costs spiked.
item.content:encoded:
Some flights are less than 100 miles, and they're the backbone of the U.S. air system, linking small towns and big cities. But short routes were in decline even before jet fuel costs spiked.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Is the U.S. slipping into 'Competitive Authoritarianism?'
item.description: What form of government do we have in America now? Some scholars say it is no longer liberal democracy, but "competitive authoritarianism." NPR's Frank Langfitt explains the term and its origin.
item.content:encoded:
What form of government do we have in America now? Some scholars say it is no longer liberal democracy, but "competitive authoritarianism." NPR's Frank Langfitt explains the term and its origin.

(Image credit: Fabrice Coffrini)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
item.description: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.
item.content:encoded:
A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.

(Image credit: Michel Euler)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: $4.8M settlement reached over Louisiana traffic stop death, AP sources say
item.description: Louisiana officials have agreed to a tentative $4.8 million settlement with the family of Ronald Greene, a Black motorist who died during a violent roadside arrest carried out by five white officers.
item.content:encoded:
Louisiana officials have agreed to a tentative $4.8 million settlement with the family of Ronald Greene, a Black motorist who died during a violent roadside arrest carried out by five white officers.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's proposed 'Golden Dome' estimated to cost $1.2 trillion
item.description: A Congressional Budget Office analysis suggests the cost of the missile defense program could be $1.2 trillion over the next 20 years, a far heftier sum than the initial $175 billion price tag.
item.content:encoded:
A Congressional Budget Office analysis suggests the cost of the missile defense program could be $1.2 trillion over the next 20 years, a far heftier sum than the initial $175 billion price tag.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
